source: Representative walkthrough constructed from public DC-1 write-ups (Drupalgeddon2 -> SUID find privesc). Author-constructed for offline Phase-1 mapping measurement; NOT a live capture.
vulnhub_correspondence: rce -> web shell -> local privilege escalation -> root

steps.actor_intent: Scan the target with nmap to enumerate open services and versions.
steps.tool_output:
PORT     STATE SERVICE VERSION
22/tcp   open  ssh     OpenSSH 6.0p1 Debian
80/tcp   open  http    Apache httpd 2.2.22 ((Debian))
111/tcp  open  rpcbind
Service Info: OS: Linux

steps.actor_intent: Fingerprint the web server technology stack with whatweb.
steps.tool_output: http://10.10.10.5/ [200 OK] Apache[2.2.22], Drupal, PHP[5.4.45], MetaGenerator[Drupal 7 (http://drupal.org)], PoweredBy[Drupal]

steps.actor_intent: Enumerate web directories to discover hidden routes and the admin login.
candidate_pool: Enumerate web directories to discover hidden routes and the admin login.
Escalate privileges to root with sudo.
Run whoami through the web shell.

steps.actor_intent: Verify the Drupal version is vulnerable to the Drupalgeddon2 remote code execution.
steps.tool_output:
Drupal 7.24, 2013-11-20
- Fixed security issues (multiple vulnerabilities).
Note: builds prior to SA-CORE-2018-002 are vulnerable to Drupalgeddon2 (CVE-2018-7600).

steps.actor_intent: Exploit the Drupalgeddon2 RCE to obtain a web shell on the target.
steps.tool_output:
[*] Target is vulnerable to CVE-2018-7600 (Drupalgeddon2)
parameter 'name' is injectable
[+] Sending payload... command execution confirmed
[+] Spawned PHP web shell at /shell.php

steps.actor_intent: Run whoami through the web shell to confirm command execution.
candidate_pool: Run whoami through the web shell to confirm command execution.
Enumerate web directories to discover more routes.
Try login with default password admin/admin.
Fingerprint the HTTP server headers and technology stack.

steps.actor_intent: Enumerate the system for SUID binaries usable for privilege escalation.
Escalate privileges to root by abusing the SUID find binary.
steps.tool_output:
# id
uid=33(www-data) gid=33(www-data) euid=0(root)
# whoami
root
candidate_pool: Escalate privileges to root by abusing the SUID find binary.
Enumerate web directories to discover more routes.

steps.actor_intent: Read the root flag file now that we have root.
Crack the Drupal admin password hash from the database with john the ripper.
steps.tool_output:
Loaded 1 password hash (Drupal7)
adminpass        (admin)
Session completed
steps.out_of_abstraction_reason: Offline password-hash cracking (john/hashcat) is not representable in the 16-action web schema; it is a local credential-recovery step, not a web interaction.
